title: Intensely Buttery Korean Beef Muchim
description: A quick-fix Korean dish featuring tender beef, buttery chimichurri sauce, and paleo-friendly ingredients, all pressure-cooked to perfection in a muchim form.
instructions: Press the saute button on the pressure cooker and melt 2 tablespoons of butter until foamy.
Add the sliced beef to the pressure cooker and cook until browned, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pressure cooker and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chimichurri sauce, coconut aminos, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper to the pressure cooker, stirring to combine.
Close the lid of the pressure cooker and set the valve to sealing, then cook on high pressure for 10 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 5 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter until melted and smooth.
Serve the beef muchim hot, garnished with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions if desired.
